ip_outcome_note: ip_verified was already true from prior vision pass; no IP verification task needed

trivia_bullets.text: Trickster's Elk is an Enchantment Creature in MH3 (June 14, 2024) using the Bestow keyword, originally from Theros block (2013), reimagined in a non-Greek-mythology context. MH3 revisited multiple older mechanics as callbacks to the Modern card pool.

"Wait. Is *that* one Dad?" —Rowan Kenrith, to Will [Scryfall]. Callback to Throne of Eldraine: The Wildered Quest (2019), in which Oko transformed High King Algenus Kenrith — father of Rowan and Will — into a stag. The flavor text implies the grown twins encounter another transformed creature and wonder if the trick was repeated.
sources: MTG story: Throne of Eldraine: The Wildered Quest

trivia_bullets.text: The Bestow effect mirrors Oko, Thief of Crowns' +1 ability (target creature becomes a 3/3 green Elk and loses all abilities) but as a one-shot aura rather than a repeatable planeswalker ability. Oko, Thief of Crowns was banned in Standard, Pioneer, Historic, Brawl, and Legacy. EDHREC rank 5,602 indicates moderate Commander play.
sources: Scryfall: Oko, Thief of Crowns legalities

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Karl Kopinski, a Nottingham-born illustrator with 192+ MTG card credits (Kresh the Bloodbraided, Teferi's Protection). Career began at Games Workshop on Warhammer 40,000 before joining MTG in the 2000s.
sources: Gatherer: artist search Karl Kopinski — 192 cards
Artsology Blog: Who Is Karl Kopinski, 2025

trivia_bullets.text: Community sentiment identified Trickster's Elk as part of a 2024 Oko rehabilitation arc: after being banned in multiple formats and narratively repositioned in Outlaws of Thunder Junction, Oko was described as finally gaining community acceptance, with this card cited as a palatable version of his most hated ability.
sources: Dexerto: Modern Horizons 3 card imitates famously broken Planeswalker Oko, 2024
